_note: Données brutes extraites du wiki (backrooms-wiki.wikidot.com), EN. Source de vérité pour le générateur scripts/import/generate.mjs. connectionsTo = TOUS les niveaux référencés ; le générateur ne garde que ceux qui existent dans le set.

levels.title: Habitable Zone
levels.shortDescription: Vast warehouse-like level of concrete halls with non-Euclidean geometry, periodic blackouts, and crates of survival supplies.
levels.atmosphere: Abandoned industrial complex: flickering fluorescents, distant pipe sounds, disorienting architecture and eerie silence.
levels.tips: Navigate by landmarks against false distances. Cross corridors during Flickering events. Loot crates and join group bases.

levels.title: Abandoned Utility Halls
levels.shortDescription: Infinite but mappable maze of Euclidean maintenance tunnels full of machinery, piping and buzzing fluorescent light.
levels.atmosphere: Industrial and claustrophobic: tattered concrete, quiet whirring machines, and darkness spreading after a recent blackout.
levels.tips: Shelter near Office Space EL3A. Mark doors; series-wired lights black out whole sections. Never open locked void rooms.

levels.title: Electrical Station
levels.shortDescription: A dangerous old electrical facility of brick corridors and machinery: resource-rich but defended by unusually intelligent entities.
levels.atmosphere: Dim brick passages, humming equipment and black pipes, alternating cramped tunnels and vast oppressive industrial halls.
levels.tips: Avoid dark paranoia-inducing corridors. Shelter in angel-statue sanctums. Never try to pass the indestructible bars.

levels.title: Abandoned Office
levels.shortDescription: An empty office building with blacked-out windows: a major hub where wanderers gather, restock and prepare to go deeper.
levels.atmosphere: Quiet corporate spaces, abandoned furniture and fluorescent light, calm near the water dispensers but eerily empty.
levels.tips: Stock Almond Water before advancing. Avoid clear (non-blacked-out) windows — they are traps. Several easy exits for retreat.

levels.title: Terror Hotel
levels.shortDescription: An infinite 1930s hotel of mahogany halls and self-cleaning vintage rooms, haunted by whispers and clouds of deadly insects.
levels.atmosphere: Pristine early-20th-century luxury turned unsettling: random smooth jazz, watching portraits, phantom taps in empty halls.
levels.tips: Avoid dark rooms where insects nest. Stay in furnished areas. Never use maintenance elevators or go above floor 12.

levels.title: Lights Out
levels.shortDescription: A pitch-black level of endless tight smooth-concrete hallways where total darkness and silence wear down the mind.
levels.atmosphere: Absolute darkness and silence breed paranoia; auditory hallucinations of scuttling, breathing and whispering are common.
levels.tips: Navigate by touch. Never flip a found light switch. Avoid the Mimicry. Follow the sound of waves toward the Level 7 exit.

levels.title: Thalassophobia
levels.shortDescription: An impossibly vast ocean under a concrete ceiling, split into zones that grow darker, colder and deadlier with depth.
levels.atmosphere: Frigid desolate underwater expanse, skeletal remains on the seafloor, dim ambient light fading into hostile black depths.
levels.tips: Use the furnished entrance as base camp. Exploit each entity's weakness (light, then hearing) to slip between territories.

levels.title: Cave Systems
levels.shortDescription: A sprawling non-Euclidean cave network with treacherous terrain, wild temperature and gravity swings, and thin air.
levels.atmosphere: Dark claustrophobic chambers of chaotic rock, cold stagnant air, distant creature sounds and constant risk of disorientation.
levels.tips: Stay on the M.E.G.-marked 9th Road. Keep moving against hypothermia, watch oxygen, avoid spider colonies and floating rocks.

levels.title: The Suburbs
levels.shortDescription: An infinite midnight suburb of furnished houses and deadly streets, resource-bearing but swarming with hostile entities.
levels.atmosphere: A neighborhood frozen at night: fog, dead trees, static-like mental interference and a pervasive sense of being watched.
levels.tips: Avoid the entity-spawning fog. Keep to sidewalks and roads. Loot houses warily and follow arrow signs toward Level 11.

levels.title: Bumper Crop
levels.shortDescription: Endless wheat and barley fields under a permanent overcast sky, dotted with empty farm buildings and worm-riddled soil.
levels.atmosphere: Dreary unchanging daylight with drifting rain and fog over cultivated fields, blurring any sense of passing time.
levels.tips: Never dig deeper than a meter or the worms surface. Follow the dirt roads to find exits. Eat the wheat only sparingly.
